title: Clade II Mpox Infections Among Cruise Ship Passengers and Crew Members â United States, 2024 | MMWR
text:
ruise lines should consider educating crew members on symptoms, risks, and preventive measures related to mpox and working with medical personnel to facilitate mpox vaccination as preexposure prophylaxis for eligible crew members, to help protect them from infection and reduce risk for potential onboard exposures. Cruise passengers who are eligible should receive mpox vaccine before cruise travel, with the second dose administered at least 2 weeks before travel to optimize effectiveness ( 8 ). Persons receiving mpox vaccine should be informed that infections might occur despite vaccination but could be less severe. For cruise voyages marketed to gay and bisexual men, having mpox vaccine available on board would facilitate timely PEP, if indicated; disseminating mpox prevention messaging and education ††† to passengers and crew members before and during a voyage are also recommended.
